steps.title: Read the trend around 60%
steps.content: The scatterplot has an increasing trend. At $60\%$ concentration, the predicted density should be between the nearby trend values around $53\%$ and $67\%$.

steps.title: Estimate from nearby values
steps.content: The trend is about $1{,}255$ near $53\%$ and about $1{,}340$ near $67\%$. Since $60\%$ is roughly halfway between those concentrations, the prediction should be near the middle of those densities, about $1{,}300$.

steps.title: Predict density at 60% and match a choice
steps.content: Of the choices $1{,}200$, $1{,}250$, $1{,}300$, and $1{,}350$, the closest prediction is $1{,}300$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
